summary:
Giardia lamblia is characterized by the production of persistent cysts detectable in stool microscopy, which can survive in the external environment for weeks, explaining their common appearance in feces even during periods of low infection intensity. These environmentally resilient cysts contribute significantly to transmission cycles and make them frequently identifiable via standard microscopic examination. \boxed{C}  

Note: Only two sentences as required; focuses exclusively on clinically significant features related to detection in stool samples.
